blocks.content:
## The AI Code-Gen Landscape

*From demo magic to product category*

Orient yourself in the prompt-to-code market. Understand the competitive architecture of v0, Lovable, Bolt, and Replit Agent; the prototype-to-production gap; and where Figma Make sits in the ecosystem.

## Modules in This Level

### 1.1 The Prompt-to-Code Product Category

The AI coding market is genuinely confusing because the output of many products looks similar — you type a prompt, you get code — while the architecture, user intent, and business model underneath dif

### 1.2 Competitive Architecture Map

The surface similarity of these products obscures completely different bets on runtime architecture, isolation model, deployment target, and agent design. A Platform PM who cannot articulate these dif

### 1.3 The Prototype-to-Production Gap

This is the core tension of the Platform PM role. Make's demo magic is real — the speed, the quality, the "I can't believe I built that" moment. But the distance between that demo and a production app

### 1.4 User Segments and Their Failure Modes

The hardest product design challenge in platform work is serving multiple user segments simultaneously without degrading the experience for any of them. Make's three segments have incompatible mental 

### 1.5 Figma Make's Position

Positioning is not marketing — it is a statement of where you are structurally advantaged and where you are structurally behind. Make's Platform PM must hold both sides of this analysis simultaneously
